video_title: Splinter Cell: Deathwatch | Official Teaser | Netflix
comment_text:
This does not look great. Just my opinion here, but the draw for me to Splinter Cell and other Tom Clancy games of the older era was believability and immersion. The nomenclature sounded like stuff a black ops intel team might use. The tools and methods were believable. Sam would and should do just about anything to avoid a gunfight because he's just one guy, so like in real life, it'd be about the worst case scenario. Tension, when you let it actually build and then release, there's a lot more of a rush because it doesn't have that this-is-just-the-thousandth-guy-ive-killed-without-even-trying-so-no-big-deal-because-im-so-badass vibe. It's not, aiming a pistol at someone with a rifle who already has the drop on you then them being all shocked like some goofy anime character while the protagonist makes sure looks as cool as possible while taking a one handed pistol shot at over 100 yards.

I really hope I'm wrong but I'm seeing a lot of just nostalgia (familiar noises/gadgets) mixed in with bland "badass" action sequences. I hope they at least put some actual tradecraft stuff in there. Sam is supposed to clever, disciplined, effective. A strong warrior, but a thinking one.

If they make him a John Wick or anime style "legend" doing basically gun katas, I'm going to be severely disappointed.

video_title: Splinter Cell: Deathwatch | Official Teaser | Netflix
comment_text:
Annoys me they have john wick writer doing it....an action writer 🤦‍♂️
Get a good thriller writer if you want to emphasise the tension and stealth but that's too much to ask for I guess. Easier to make it a dumb action movie that is "splinter cell" in name only.

I'll watch it to see if I'm wrong (a man can dream) but it looks like a braindead action show instead of a tense spy thriller with stealth at the forefront, ya know the thing the character and the entire namesake of the games are known for 🤷‍♂️

I suspect Ironside was asked to do it and he saw how the script completely misunderstood the core DNA of the original IP and said no since it opted to be an action show over a stealth thriller.

comment_text: They couldn’t make open world splinter cell yet they have Ghost recon breakpoint and Watch dogs. Talk about being creatively *inept on purpose. I just think someone at UbiSoft either saw too much woke crap and didn’t want to hire for a woke splinter cell or they’re just not creative enough to put the obvious together yes you can’t climb poles in the middle of a desert that’s why you have Urban terrain (watch dogs). The Watchdogs engine alone offers a lot of tools already for a proper Splinter cell game. That’s just me though. I think there’s a creativity lapse at Ubi or just activism masked as silent bureaucracy. Especially in 2025 this is the perfect time to announce a game (gamescom 2025 missed opportunity ).

video_title: Splinter Cell: Deathwatch | Official Teaser | Netflix
comment_text:
Splinter Cell was stealth, infiltration and finesse even when unhinged. 
The writer of John Wick, the complete opposite then.
A mid-air double headshot as an ageing fisher doesn’t seem canon or celebrated added technical flair as a choice.
Michael Ironside is the only voice I want to hear non live action.
Everyone deserves a chance, everyone can do multiple things. Im all for the world being used but please, if it sucks then allow the fans to guide your hand as we still want more of that world. Just true to the canon and gaming canon.
